You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/console/utils/single-typeahead-dropdown.tsx

### Fix 1
Line: 364
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: plain

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'


Rule [family:Select]:
## Select Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Select activeItemId={...} activeMenu={...} containsDrilldown={...} containsFlyout={...} drilldownItemPath={...} drilledInMenus={...} focusTimeoutDelay={...} id={...}>
    <SelectList isAriaMultiselectable={...}>
      <SelectOption actions={...} component={...} description={...} direction={...} download={...} drilldownMenu={...} flyoutMenu={...} hasCheckbox={...}>
        <SelectList />
        <Select />
      </SelectOption>
    </SelectList>
    <SelectGroup innerRef={...} label={...} labelHeadingLevel={...} titleId={...} />
  </Select>
```

Props that stay on <Select>: activeItemId, activeMenu, containsDrilldown, containsFlyout, drilldownItemPath, drilledInMenus, focusTimeoutDelay, id, innerRef, isMenuDrilledIn, isNavFlyout, isOpen, isPlain, isRootMenu, isScrollable, maxMenuHeight, menuHeight, onActionClick, onDrillIn, onDrillOut, onGetMenuHeight, onOpenChange, onOpenChangeKeys, onSelect, onToggleKeydown, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, parentMenu, popperProps, role, selected, shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen, shouldFocusToggleOnSelect, shouldPreventScrollOnItemFocus, toggle, variant, zIndex

### Fix 3
Line: 363
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: TextInputGroupUtilities

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

```

  363              <Button
  364                variant="plain"

```

### Fix 4
Line: 327
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuToggle
Prop: icon

```
  328        ref={toggleRef}

```

### Fix 5
Line: 335
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-textinputgroupmain]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <TextInputGroupMain> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</TextInputGroupMain>

Incident context:

### Fix 6
Line: 10
Rule [sd-test-select-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Select is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Select renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Select may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Select
  localName: Select
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
    9    MenuToggleElement,
   10    Select,
   11    SelectProps,

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/console/utils/single-typeahead-dropdown.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Line 364):** `Button variant="plain"` — The type changed to add `'stateful'` but `"plain"` is still valid. No code change needed.

**Fix 2 (Line 381):** Select family migration. Two sub-incidents:
- Line 381: `onSelect` type changed from `(event?, value?: string | number)` to `(event?, value?: SelectOptionProps['value'])`. The current `onSelect` handler on line 199-214 uses `value: string | number | undefined`. Need to update the type to match `SelectOptionProps['value']`.
- Line 386: `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` — This prop still exists on Select in v6 (it's listed in "Props that stay on <Select>"). The fix strategy mentions `focusTimeoutDelay` but `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` is also still valid. Let me check more carefully... The rule says the prop signature changed. Looking at the props that stay on Select, `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` is listed. So this prop is still valid. No change needed for that sub-incident.

Actually, let me re-read Fix 2 more carefully. The `onSelect` callback type changed. Currently on line 201, the parameter type is `value: string | number | undefined`. In v6 it should be `value: SelectOptionProps['value']`. Since `SelectOptionProps` is already imported, I should update the `onSelect` handler's signature.

**Fix 3 (Line 363):** Button icon-children-to-prop. The Button currently has `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />` as children. Since it's an icon-only button, I should move the icon to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing.

**Fix 4 (Line 327):** MenuToggle icon-children-to-prop. Looking at the MenuToggle (lines 327-373), its children are the entire `<TextInputGroup>` block. This is NOT an icon — it's the typeahead input. This is a typeahead variant MenuToggle, so the children are the text input group, not an icon. This fix doesn't apply here since there's no icon element as a child.

Wait, let me re-read. The rule says "icon content in MenuToggle must be passed to the `icon` prop". But the MenuToggle here has `variant="typeahead"` and its children are a `TextInputGroup`, not an icon. There's no icon being passed as children to MenuToggle directly. The fix doesn't apply to this particular usage pattern.

**Fix 5 (Line 335):** TextInputGroupMain icon-children-to-prop. Looking at lines 335-360, TextInputGroupMain is self-closing (ends with `/>` on line 360). It has no children. No icon children to move.

**Fix 6 (Line 10):** Select behavioral change note about Popper `appendTo` default change. This is informational — the code doesn't directly reference Popper or its `appendTo` prop. No code change needed, but I should be aware that Select's dropdown may now portal to `document.body` by default.

Let me now apply the changes:

1. **Fix 1**: No change needed — `variant="plain"` is still valid.
2. **Fix 2**: Update `onSelect` handler parameter type from `string | number | undefined` to `SelectOptionProps['value']`. The `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` prop is still valid.
3. **Fix 3**: Move `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />` from Button children to `icon` prop.
4. **Fix 4**: No change needed — MenuToggle children are TextInputGroup (typeahead), not an icon.
5. **Fix 5**: No change needed — TextInputGroupMain has no icon children.
6. **Fix 6**: No code change needed — informational about Popper behavioral change.

arguments.after:
<Button
              variant="plain"
              onClick={onClearButtonClick}
              aria-label={t('Clear input value')}
              icon={<TimesIcon aria-hidden />}
            />
arguments.before:
<Button
              variant="plain"
              onClick={onClearButtonClick}
              aria-label={t('Clear input value')}
            >
              <TimesIcon aria-hidden />
            </Button>

content.text: Now let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct with all applicable changes applied. Let me also verify there are no downstream type issues with the `selectOption` function which receives the `value` from `onSelect`. The `selectOption` function on line 191 takes `value: string | number`, and inside `onSelect` the value is passed as either `filterValue` (a string) or `value` directly. Since `SelectOptionProps['value']` can be `string | number | undefined`, the existing usage where we guard with `value && value !== NO_RESULTS` before passing to `selectOption` remains compatible — the truthy check ensures it's not undefined, and the type is still compatible with `string | number`.

Now let me check line 212 — `selectOption(value, ...)` where `value` could now be `SelectOptionProps['value']`. The `selectOption` function expects `string | number`. Since `SelectOptionProps['value']` is typically `string | number | undefined` and we've already checked `value` is truthy on line 210, this is safe.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant="plain"` type change
**No change needed.** The value `"plain"` remains valid in the updated type union (`'stateful'` was added, nothing was removed).

### Fix 2 — Select family migration (`onSelect` type change + `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen`)
**Applied.** Updated the `onSelect` callback parameter type on line 201 from `value: string | number | undefined` to `value: SelectOptionProps['value'] | undefined` to match the v6 signature. The `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` prop is still valid in v6 (listed in "Props that stay on Select"), so no change needed for that sub-incident.

### Fix 3 — Button icon-children-to-prop
**Applied.** Moved `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />` from Button children to the `icon` prop and made the Button element self-closing: `<Button variant="plain" ... icon={<TimesIcon aria-hidden />} />`.

### Fix 4 — MenuToggle icon-children-to-prop
**No change needed.** The MenuToggle has `variant="typeahead"` and its children are a `<TextInputGroup>` (the typeahead input), not an icon element. This fix pattern does not apply to typeahead toggle variants.

### Fix 5 — TextInputGroupMain icon-children-to-prop
**No change needed.** The `<TextInputGroupMain>` element is already self-closing (`/>`) with no children, so there is no icon content to move.

### Fix 6 — Select behavioral change (Popper `appendTo` default)
**No change needed.** This is an informational/awareness fix about Popper's `appendTo` default changing from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. The code does not directly configure Popper's `appendTo`, so no code change is required. If dropdown positioning issues arise at runtime, `popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }}` can be added to the `<Select>` to restore the old behavior.

  Before: <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</TextInputGroupMain>

### Fix 1 — `Button.variant="plain"` type change
**No change needed.** The value `"plain"` remains valid in the updated type union (`'stateful'` was added, nothing was removed).

The `shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen` prop is still valid in v6 (listed in "Props that stay on Select"), so no change needed for that sub-incident.

### Fix 3 — Button icon-children-to-prop
**Applied.** Moved `<TimesIcon aria-hidden />` from Button children to the `icon` prop and made the Button element self-closing: `<Button variant="plain" ... icon={<TimesIcon aria-hidden />} />`.

### Fix 4 — MenuToggle icon-children-to-prop
**No change needed.** The MenuToggle has `variant="typeahead"` and its children are a `<TextInputGroup>` (the typeahead input), not an icon element. This fix pattern does not apply to typeahead toggle variants.

### Fix 5 — TextInputGroupMain icon-children-to-prop
**No change needed.** The `<TextInputGroupMain>` element is already self-closing (`/>`) with no children, so there is no icon content to move.

### Fix 6 — Select behavioral change (Popper `appendTo` default)
**No change needed.** This is an informational/awareness fix about Popper's `appendTo` default changing from `'inline'` to `() => document.body`. The code does not directly configure Popper's `appendTo`, so no code change is required. If dropdown positioning issues arise at runtime, `popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }}` can be added to the `<Select>` to restore the old behavior.
